---
title: "APN+ Bulk Connectivity"
subtitle: "Give us a messy list. Get back warm-intro decisions you can explain."
date: "2026-06-19"
author: "Frank · narrative direction by $don"
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    number-sections: true
    theme: cosmo
    title-block-banner: true
    smooth-scroll: true
    embed-resources: true
callout-appearance: simple
execute:
  echo: false
---

::: {.callout-note appearance="default"}
## Report status
A derived planning report. It summarizes the current APN+ Bulk Search / Bulk
Connectivity corpus and does **not** replace the underlying Jira, Confluence,
Slack, Outlook, and legacy-app evidence.
:::

# The decision {.unnumbered}

::: {.callout-tip appearance="default"}
## The one question this product answers
> **Given this list of companies or people — who can Advent credibly reach,
> through whom, and with what evidence?**
:::

Everything below serves that one question.
[**$steve:** strongest open in the doc — the single question earns its place. Keep it.]{.aside}

Today the product is called **Bulk Search**. That name describes a mechanism:
you search many things at once. But searching was never the job. The job is
taking a messy list of companies or people, resolving it against Advent data,
generating APN connectivity, and helping someone choose a warm-intro path they
can *explain* in a room.

So we change the conversation.

:::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="48%"}
### What it's called today
**Bulk Search** — a mechanism.
A way to look up many items.
:::
::: {.column width="48%"}
### What it actually is
**Bulk Connectivity** — an outcome.
A way to turn a list into trusted intro decisions.
:::
::::

It's not a search tool. It's a connectivity tool. That single reframe is the
spine of the redesign.

::: {.callout-warning collapse="true"}
## 💬 $steve · critique
The before/after columns land. But the reframe rides on a naming decision that
isn't approved yet — don't let the deck *imply* it's settled. The gap callout at
the bottom covers us; consider a one-word "(proposed)" next to the new name here
so a skimmer doesn't miss it.
:::

## Keep what already works

The legacy concepts are good. The redesign changes how they're *organized and
explained*, not whether they exist:

- company lists;
- people lists;
- Advent Connectivity generation;
- generated-list lineage;
- sharing and read-only states;
- subscriptions;
- downloads and exports;
- Company Screening;
- APN profile actions.

# The product, in one frame

| Dimension | Working answer |
| --- | --- |
| Product | APN+ Bulk Connectivity |
| Legacy label | Bulk Search |
| Primary user | Advent deal teams, APN-heavy users, operating partners, and data-quality/support users |
| Business goal | Turn Advent's relationship network into faster, more trusted list-based workflows |
| Primary workflow | Paste/upload list → resolve matches → save source list → generate connectivity → review output → inspect evidence → act |
| Current problem | Useful legacy behavior is buried in a busy list manager and wide result grids |
| Product promise | Give APN+ a messy list and get back trusted, traceable warm-intro decisions |

The whole workflow, at one glance:

```{mermaid}
flowchart LR
  A[Paste / upload list] --> B[Resolve matches]
  B --> C[Save source list]
  C --> D[Generate connectivity]
  D --> E[Review output]
  E --> F[Inspect evidence]
  F --> G[Act: intro / share / export]
```

# Why this matters for Advent

Advent's network is an operating advantage. But an advantage only compounds when
people can **find it, trust it, and act on it quickly.** Right now there's a gap
between *"I have a list"* and *"I know who can help."* This product closes it.

Instead of searching one company or person at a time, a user can ask:

:::: {layout-ncol=2}
::: {.callout-note appearance="minimal"}
**Reach**
Which target companies have the strongest Advent connectivity?
:::
::: {.callout-note appearance="minimal"}
**People**
Which people around those companies are actually reachable?
:::
::: {.callout-note appearance="minimal"}
**Path**
Which banker, advisor, portfolio exec, or Advent colleague is the best path?
:::
::: {.callout-note appearance="minimal"}
**Signal**
Which list should be monitored for future signals?
:::
::::

And then: which output can be shared or exported for a deal discussion?

# Product goals

:::: {.panel-tabset}

## 1 · Reframe around connectivity

Use **Bulk Connectivity** as the product framing *if Sumit approves.* Preserve
`/bulk-search` and the old label for route compatibility and discoverability.

Why it matters, plainly:

- `Bulk Search` describes a **mechanism**.
- `Bulk Connectivity` describes the **Advent business outcome**.

[The reframe is a naming decision with a real dependency — **not yet approved**; see the open decisions below.]{.aside}

## 2 · Make list creation reliable

Users should paste or upload rows and understand *what happened* before the
product saves or mutates a list. No silent mutations.

Show the user:

- accepted identifiers;
- row count;
- row limits;
- parsed fields;
- field mapping;
- match status;
- unresolved rows;
- duplicate rows;
- failed rows;
- save or continue options.

[**💬 $frank · requirements:** "row limits" needs a concrete number to be testable. Flagging, not filling — the limit should come from the ingestion spec, not the narrative.]{.aside}
[**💬 $cjay · UI:** these nine states need a single status component, not nine ad-hoc UI treatments.]{.aside}

## 3 · Make matching first-class

Matching is not a hidden backend step or an error state. It's a workflow the
user drives. The UI must distinguish:

- matched;
- review required;
- unmatched;
- duplicate;
- skipped;
- failed.

Users need to confirm, change, search, exclude, or bulk-accept matches — with
**visible confidence criteria.**

## 4 · Make connectivity generation explicit

Generating connectivity is a visible product moment — progress, status,
destination. No black box.

The user should always know:

- what source list is being used;
- what output will be created;
- what stage the job is in;
- how many rows have been processed;
- how many results were generated;
- what failed;
- where the generated list lives.

## 5 · Make results decision-first

The result grid helps users choose **who matters first.** Default columns
prioritize the scan, not the metadata:

- person or company;
- related source company;
- role or title;
- APN group;
- connection level;
- strength;
- best intro path;
- evidence freshness;
- match status;
- action.

Long evidence and source metadata belong **outside the main scan path.**

[**💬 $dieter · research:** "evidence freshness" and "strength" are the columns users will trust or distrust the product on. We have no study yet on how users read those signals — flagging as an open research question before we commit the default sort to "strength."]{.aside}

::::

# What we haven't settled yet

::: {.callout-important appearance="default"}
## Open decisions — label the gap, don't paper over it
- **Naming is not approved.** "Bulk Connectivity" depends on Sumit's sign-off.
  Until then, treat it as a proposal, not a fact.
- **No metrics in this report.** This is a planning spine derived from the
  corpus; it asserts no usage, adoption, or performance numbers. Any number
  added later must trace to Jira / Confluence / Slack / Outlook evidence.
- **Source of truth.** This document summarizes evidence; it does not replace
  it. Decisions should be checked against the underlying record.
:::
